payload.answer: Do not store the 500-row CSV table as durable memory. Large tabular/raw data should not be committed to personal memory unless it contains concise, durable, user-relevant facts. If needed, store only a brief summary, schema description, provenance, or a pointer/reference to the external dataset, not all rows.

entity_resolution.reason: The input is a large repetitive CSV-style table and does not contain durable personal facts, preferences, identity information, or stable user-relevant knowledge suitable for memory.

details: Raw large table rejected for durable memory storage.
Recommended summarization or external reference instead.

repair_options: Ask the user whether they want a summary of the table stored instead of the raw rows.
Store only metadata such as: CSV with columns id, name, value; approximately 500 rows; repetitive content.
Store a reference or file pointer if an external storage mechanism is available.
Extract and store only durable user-relevant facts if the table contains any.
